steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Left side: independent clause (ending with “he authored many of his works”)
Right side: independent clause (beginning with “among these personas was Ricardo Reis...”)

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon joins two related independent clauses. Choice B correctly uses a semicolon between them. The other three violate this rule: A redundantly pairs a colon with the conjunction and, C uses and without proper parallel structure, and D uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. That matches choice B — B is correct.
